data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260514T185436-8a6d9fe4/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260514T185436-8a6d9fe4/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260514T185436-8a6d9fe4/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260514T185436-8a6d9fe4/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260514T185436-8a6d9fe4/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-8a7a6843">
    <title>execute-loop: surface and act on ladder-exhausted escalation instead of silently no-op'ing</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
On provider_connectivity failure, applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion calls nextFloorFn(actualPower). When the configured power ladder has no step above actualPower (common when the local config exposes only cheap-tier providers), nextFloorFn returns powerladder.ErrLadderExhausted. The current code silently swallows that error and does NOT set b.Extra[TriagePowerHintKey]. As a result the next claim sees no hint, the routing intent re-derives "cheap", and the same dead (provider, model) is selected again. The failed-route list is appended to but never consulted by Fizeau, so it has no effect on the next decision. A drain in this state attempts each bead once, times out for ~2m45s in Fizeau retries, gets the same provider_connectivity outcome, and bottoms out at 100% failures with no escalation ever taking effect. Reproduces exactly when local LM Studio hosts (bragi/grendel) are unreachable: every bead routes to power=5 cheap models, fails, retry-after expires 15min later, re-routes to the same model, fails again.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3862-3866 in applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion:
    nextFloor, err := nextFloorFn(report.ActualPower)
    if err != nil {
        return
    }
    b.Extra[TriagePowerHintKey] = nextFloor

The early return swallows ErrLadderExhausted. The same silent-swallow pattern also lives in applyNoChangesSmartRetry (execute_bead_loop.go:3454) and applyRepairCycleExhaustedEscalation (execute_bead_loop.go:3497). The bead-event log gets a route-failure entry but no operator-visible signal that escalation aborted.

PROPOSED FIX
- When nextFloorFn returns ErrLadderExhausted (or any error), emit a kind=execution-escalation-aborted event with a summary naming the failed (provider, model, actualPower) and the reason "ladder exhausted at power N".
- Set TriagePowerHintKey to actualPower+1 anyway (or to a sentinel >=actualPower+1) so the next route decision at least knows "anything but the failed tier" — this is a no-op for routers that ignore the hint, but it gives clear signal to Fizeau-side exclusion honoring (separate bead).
- After 2 consecutive provider_connectivity failures on the SAME (provider, model), promote the bead's outcome to operator_required with a clear message ("provider X / model Y unreachable on 2+ attempts; check ddx config or provider health") so the queue does not silently re-loop forever.
- Apply the same change to applyNoChangesSmartRetry and applyRepairCycleExhaustedEscalation so all three escalation paths surface ladder exhaustion uniformly.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau-side routing exclusion (separate bead in upstream repo).
- Do not introduce a new bead status; reuse open/blocked/operator_required.
- Do not auto-edit operator config; just signal clearly.

DEPS
None.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestApplyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion_LadderExhaustedEmitsEvent asserts a kind=execution-escalation-aborted event is appended when nextFloorFn returns ErrLadderExhausted, and that summary names provider, model, and actualPower.
2. TestApplyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion_LadderExhaustedSetsSentinelHint asserts TriagePowerHintKey is set to a value >= actualPower+1 even when ladder is exhausted (so downstream routing has explicit "not the failed tier" signal).
3. TestProviderConnectivityRepeatedFailure_PromotesToOperatorRequired asserts that after 2 consecutive provider_connectivity failures against the same (provider, model), the bead's outcome_reason transitions to operator_required with a message naming the unreachable provider.
4. TestApplyNoChangesSmartRetry_LadderExhaustedEmitsEvent and TestApplyRepairCycleExhaustedEscalation_LadderExhaustedEmitsEvent assert the same signaling on the other two escalation paths.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:routing, area:execution, kind:bug, phase:2, spec:endpoint-first-routing</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="cfad0fbd83361dbb7b6d793e5074fe723fd385f5" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260514T185436-8a6d9fe4"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All AC criteria are satisfied. Here's a summary of the changes committed in `aae733613`:

**Files changed:**

- `cli/internal/bead/park.go` — Added `ParkProviderConnectivityRepeated` park reason (used when bead escalates after 2+ connectivity failures)
- `cli/internal/bead/store_test.go` — Updated exhaustive ParkReason test to include the new constant
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`:
  - Added `emitEscalationAbortedEvent` helper that records `kind=execution-escalation-aborted` events naming provider, model, and power
  - `applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion`: added `actor` param; on ladder exhaustion emits the event and sets `TriagePowerHintKey = actualPower+1` sentinel; on repeat failure for same (provider, model) parks to `operator_required` via `ParkToProposed`
  - `applyNoChangesSmartRetry`: emits escalation-aborted event before parking when ladder is exhausted
  - `applyRepairCycleExhaustedEscalation`: emits escalation-aborted event before parking when ladder is exhausted
  - Updated the call site (line 2275) to pass `assignee` as actor
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_escalation_aborted_test.go` — New file with all 5 AC tests (all pass)

The pre-existing `TestBeadLifecycleSkill*` failures (missing `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` in worktree) are not caused by this bead and pre-date this change.
